revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:伊波乃西神社]] {{Citation needed|date=September 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Iwanonishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Iwanonishi jinja2008-1.jpg | location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, [[:en:Gifu City|Gifu City]], Iwata Nishi 3-chome, 421 | coordinates={{Coord|35.4469528|N|136.8350111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Hikoimasu|qid=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}} | Shrine Rank=[[Engishiki Shrine|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[Gōsha]]
[[Kinpei-sha|Ginpei-sha]] | Established=Unknown | Main Hall= | festival= | Rituals=Imperial Mausoleum Festival (March 21) | prefecture={{ill|Gifu|qid=Q45798|1=Gifu}} }} '''Iwanonishi Shrine''' (伊波乃西神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Gifu City|Gifu City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} (formerly part of {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}, [[Kakamigun|Kakami District]]). It is classified as a [[Shikinaisha|shrine listed in the Engishiki]]. Adjacent to the shrine is the "Hikozaka no Mikoto Tomb." == Overview == * The date of establishment is unknown. * '''[[Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|Hikozaka no Mikoto]]''' is the third prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], the half-brother of [[:en:Sujin Tennō|Sujin Tennō]], the great-grandfather of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, the father of [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]], and the grandfather of [[Itonishikiiribiko no Mikoto]] (enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|en|Inaba Shrine|qid=Q701414|lt=Inaba Shrine|1=Inaba Shrine}}). He is traditionally said to have governed the {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}} along with his son, '''Yatsuri no Irihiko''' ([[Kamioone no Mikoto]]), focusing on infrastructure development such as irrigation and land cultivation. It is said he passed away in this area, where he was buried. '''Yatsuri no Irihiko''' is regarded as the ancestor of the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] ([[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]]), though Kakamigun was part of the [[Minogo Kuni no Miyatsuko]]’s territory. * The adjacent "Hikozaka no Mikoto Tomb" is located at the base of Mount Shimizu and was once a natural stone called "Iwazaki-sama." In 1875, a survey by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} determined it as the tomb of Hikozaka no Mikoto, transferring its jurisdiction to the Imperial Mausoleum Bureau of the Imperial Household Agency. Consequently, the shrine was relocated to its current location. == Deities == * [[Hikozaka no Mikoto]] * [[Yatsuri no Irihiko no Mikoto]] == Rituals == * Imperial Mausoleum Festival: Held annually on March 21. == location == * Iwata Nishi 3-chome, 421, [[:en:Gifu City|Gifu City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} == Access == {{Commonscat|Iwanonishi-jinja}} Accessible by {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|qid=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}}, a 15-minute walk from the "Iwata" bus stop. * From [[JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal]] (north side of {{ill|Gifu Station|en|Gifu Station|qid=Q1144885|lt=Gifu Station|1=Gifu Station}}), platform 14: ** Ōhōdō Danchi Line bound for "Ōhō Midoridanchi (via Kitaisshiki)" ** Hachiman Line bound for "Hachiman Bus Depot (via Kitaisshiki)" ** Gifu Kaminosho Line bound for "Seki Higashiyama," "Gifu University of Medical Science," or "Kaminosho Kawai" ** Semizuka Daitaira Town Line bound for "Daitaira Town" * From [[Gifu Bus Terminal]] (south of {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|en|Meitetsu Gifu Station|qid=Q1109902|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Station|1=Meitetsu Gifu Station}}), platform C: ** Ōhōdō Danchi Line bound for "Ōhō Midoridanchi (via Kitaisshiki)" ** Hachiman Line bound for "Hachiman Bus Depot (via Kitaisshiki)" ** Gifu Kaminosho Line bound for "Seki Higashiyama," "Gifu University of Medical Science," or "Kaminosho Kawai" ** Semizuka Daitaira Town Line bound for "Daitaira Town" {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|伊波乃西神社|version=104353037|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Iwanonishi jinja2008-1.jpg|center|250px]] [[ja:伊波乃西神社]] {{Commons category|Iwanonishi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11379658}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
